overview.description: AI Governance Evidence Platform — forensic safety layer for AI agents with cryptographic, immutable audit trails
overview.description_long: Cynsta AI™ is the first Forensic Safety Layer for AI Agents. It provides cryptographic evidence for AI compliance, built for the EU AI Act. The platform creates immutable, court-ready proof of what AI systems do through tamper-evident logs, qualified timestamps, and a non-gateway observer architecture. Originally launched as an LLM CI tool (tests, runtime guardrails, unified logging, and Flight Recorder), it has evolved into a deep-tech AI governance and evidence platform for the agentic era. Powered by EuroHPC Leonardo and E2B.dev.
overview.tagline: We make AI agents safe to deploy.

history.origin_story: Founded in Prague in 2025 by Martin Pagac (CEO) and Michael Treml (Co-Founder) to build trust infrastructure for the digital economy. Began as an LLM CI/CD and guardrails tool, then pivoted to become an AI governance and forensic safety layer focused on EU AI Act compliance.

key_milestones.event: Received EuroHPC Joint Undertaking grant (Leonardo supercomputer access)

metadata.notes: Very early-stage startup founded in 2025. 4 employees on LinkedIn. Has two domains: cynsta.com (original LLM CI branding) and cynsta.ai (newer EU AI Act compliance branding). Pre-seed funding round exists but amount is obfuscated on Crunchbase. EuroHPC grant confirms EU-backed research/compute support.
